question: Does the model select Option B as the correct answer (or explicitly state that the linear velocity of ball 'a' is greater than that of ball 'b', v_a > v_b)?
Does the model correctly conclude that the angular velocities of the two balls are equal (ω_a = ω_b)?
Does the model correctly conclude that the linear velocity of ball 'a' is greater than that of ball 'b' (v_a > v_b)?
Does the model correctly conclude that the centripetal force on ball 'a' is greater than on ball 'b' (F_a > F_b)?
Does the model correctly conclude that the centripetal acceleration of ball 'a' is greater than that of ball 'b' (a_a > a_b)?
Does the model correctly identify the forces acting on the ball as Gravity (mg) and Tension (T), without including fictitious forces like centrifugal force in the inertial frame analysis?
Does the reasoning correctly decompose forces to establish vertical equilibrium (T cosθ = mg) and horizontal centripetal force (T sinθ = mω²r)?
Does the model correctly use the geometric relationship r = h tanθ (or equivalent trigonometry involving radius r, height h, and angle θ)?
Does the model correctly derive the expression for angular velocity as dependent on height, i.e., ω = √(g/h)?
Does the model explicitly identify that since both balls move in the same horizontal plane, their vertical height h is the same?
Does the model correctly infer that ball 'a' has a larger radius of motion than ball 'b' (r_a > r_b) based on the diagram or problem description?
Does the reasoning correctly apply the linear velocity formula v = ωr to compare the velocities of the two balls?
Does the reasoning correctly apply the centripetal force formula F = mω²r (or equivalent) considering the equal masses?
If a free-body diagram is generated, is the gravity vector (G or mg) drawn pointing vertically downward?
If a free-body diagram is generated, is the tension vector (T) drawn along the string, pointing obliquely upward toward the suspension point?
If a free-body diagram or force analysis is generated, is the resultant/centripetal force (F_net) drawn pointing horizontally toward the center of the circular path?
If a geometric diagram is generated, is the angle θ correctly marked between the string and the vertical axis?
If a geometric diagram is generated, are the radius r (horizontal from axis) and height h (vertical from suspension) correctly indicated?
If the model depicts the setup with both balls, are they shown at the same vertical level (same horizontal plane)?
If the model depicts the setup with both balls, is ball 'a' shown on a larger circular path (outer trajectory) compared to ball 'b'?
Does the diagram correctly show the strings attached to the same single suspension point on the ceiling?
Are the generated images clear enough that force vectors, geometric parameters (θ, r, h), and labels are easily distinguishable?
If the text defines specific variables (e.g., θ, T, G, r, h), do those labels appear in the generated images?
Does the text description of force directions match the vectors shown in the generated diagram (e.g., text says 'net force points to center', image shows vector pointing to center)?
Is the geometric setup in the generated image consistent with the problem description (conical pendulum, same horizontal plane, correct relative radii)?

Is there any contradiction between the text analysis and the visual representation (e.g., text says v_a > v_b but image implies otherwise, or text defines θ with vertical but image shows it with horizontal)?
